comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py:232`

This line embeds `has_blocking_indicators = any(i != "🥞" for i in indicators)` — a verbatim copy of a single expression from the source file. This will go stale if the implementation changes (e.g., additional non-blocking indicators are added).

Suggested fix: Replace with the source pointer format from `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md` (HTML comment + prose reference). A source pointer is already present on line 70 — fold the expression description into prose instead:

```markdown
The distinction is implemented in `_build_indicators()` in `lifecycle.py`: it checks whether any indicator other than 🥞 is present to determine if there are blocking indicators. When a plan is implemented and has no blocking indicators, the rocket emoji is appended to signal "ready to merge."
```

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Replaced verbatim code snippet with prose description in fd6062b8e

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 12:15 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`_build_indicators()` in `lifecycle.py`, which checks whether any indicator besides the pancake emoji (🥞) is present"
Reality: `_build_indicators()` in the current code never appends 🥞 and has no exclusion check for 🥞. The `is_stacked` parameter is accepted but unused (no-op). The blocking logic checks `is_draft`, `has_conflicts`, and `review_decision` — not the presence/absence of 🥞. Tests confirm this: see test_lifecycle_display.py:666 comment "pancake removed, stacked is no-op".

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py, _build_indicators`

Suggested fix: Update to describe the actual blocking logic — a plan is ready to merge when `is_draft is not True`, `has_conflicts is not True`, `review_decision != "CHANGES_REQUESTED"`, `checks_passing is True`, and `has_unresolved_comments is not True`. Remove the pancake-based framing entirely as the pancake indicator was removed.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in b22ee33 — corrected the description to reflect that _build_indicators() directly checks specific blocking conditions rather than using a pancake emoji exclusion check.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 02:51 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy that is now inaccurate.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py:232-233`

The doc embeds `has_blocking_indicators = any(i != "🥞" for i in indicators)`, which was the old single-emoji exclusion check. After this PR, the source now uses a multi-emoji non-blocking set:

```python
_non_blocking = {"🥞", "👀", "✔"}
has_blocking_indicators = any(i not in _non_blocking for i in indicators)
```

The 👀 (published/review) and ✔ (approved) indicators are now also non-blocking — the doc's verbatim snippet no longer matches the implementation.

Suggested fix: Replace with prose describing the expanded non-blocking set, keeping the existing source pointer on line 70:
> The distinction is implemented in `_build_indicators()` in `lifecycle.py`: it checks whether any indicator is absent from the non-blocking set (🥞, 👀, ✔). When a plan is implemented and has no blocking indicators, the rocket emoji is appended to signal "ready to merge."

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed in commit d895d6f55 — replaced verbatim code block with prose describing the expanded _non_blocking set (pancake, eyes, checkmark).

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-24 21:36 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific set membership values that will change.

Risk: The prose enumerates the exact current contents of the `_non_blocking` set (🥞, 👀, ✔). If a new non-blocking indicator is added, this description will silently go stale. The source pointer on the preceding line already points to `_build_indicators` — consider removing the specific enumeration and relying on the pointer, or add a tripwire note.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected (table below).

The table at the "Two context_for_test() Implementations" section claims `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py` has `Checks ERK_PLAN_BACKEND? → Yes — respects env var`.

Claim: `Checks ERK_PLAN_BACKEND? → Yes — respects env var`
Reality: After PR #7971, `testing.py:194` uses a tautological comparison (`"draft_pr" == "draft_pr"`) that ignores the env var entirely.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py:194`

Suggested fix: Update the table to reflect that after PR #7971, `testing.py` no longer reads `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` — the column should say `No — hardcoded to draft_pr after PR #7971`.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed: Updated table to reflect post-PR #7971 reality - erk-shared context_for_test() no longer checks ERK_PLAN_BACKEND (tautological comparison always takes draft-PR path).

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 23:11 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `"planned_pr" == "planned_pr"` always evaluates to `True`, so the planned-PR path is always taken regardless of environment variables.
Reality: The current `context_for_test()` source (testing.py:184-195) contains NO tautological comparison. The code was properly refactored — it now uses explicit conditional logic: if `plan_store` is provided use it, elif `issues_explicitly_passed` use `GitHubPlanStore`, else use `PlannedPRBackend` directly. There is no string literal comparison in the plan_store resolution block.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py:184-195`

Suggested fix: Remove the reference to `"planned_pr" == "planned_pr"`. The accurate description is: after PR #7971, `context_for_test()` defaults to `PlannedPRBackend` unless `plan_store` is explicitly provided or `issues` was explicitly passed. The env var `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` is not read at all.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in commit ef39d66 — removed all references to tautological comparison. Updated to describe the actual explicit conditional logic: defaults to PlannedPRBackend unless plan_store is explicitly provided or issues was explicitly passed.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 03:32 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — source pointer uses line numbers instead of name-based identifiers.

Source: `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py:31-33` (line-range pointer)

Risk: High maintenance burden as code evolves. Line numbers shift with any insertion above line 31. The source-pointers.md standard says to prefer name-based identifiers when the file has greppable symbol names — which it does (`_is_github_backend`).

Suggested fix:
```markdown
<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py, _is_github_backend -->
See `_is_github_backend()` in `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`.
```

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`_build_indicators()` checks whether any indicator other than 🥞 is present to determine if there are blocking indicators"
Reality: `_build_indicators()` in the current code never appends 🥞 and has no exclusion check for 🥞. The `is_stacked` parameter is accepted but is a no-op. Tests at `tests/unit/plan_store/test_lifecycle_display.py:669` explicitly document this: "Stacked PR no longer shows pancake emoji" and `_indicators(..., is_stacked=True) == "-"`.

The broader doc description of the pancake emoji (lines 11, 25-27, 38, 40, 48-53) refers to removed behavior. The stacked PR indicator (🥞) was removed from `_build_indicators()`.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py, _build_indicators`

Suggested fix: This entire document describes a feature that was removed. Either: (1) update to document that stacked PR detection still occurs in `_build_plan_row()` but the pancake indicator is no longer emitted, or (2) archive/delete the doc and remove the reference from `visual-status-indicators.md`.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in b22ee33 — corrected the description to reflect that _build_indicators() directly checks specific blocking conditions rather than using a pancake emoji exclusion check.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 02:51 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected — internal contradiction.

This line correctly notes that the pancake emoji is not currently added by `_build_indicators()`. However, this contradicts lines 27 and 52 in this same document:
- Line 27: "The pancake emoji is the first indicator added in `_build_indicators()`" — INACCURATE
- Line 52 (data flow): `→ _build_indicators appends 🥞 when is_stacked is True` — INACCURATE

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py, _build_indicators`

Reality: `_build_indicators()` never appends 🥞. The `is_stacked` parameter is a no-op. The function body has no code that reads `is_stacked` (confirmed in lifecycle.py:173-236).

Suggested fix: Update lines 27 and 52 to match the note added here. The "Indicator Ordering" section (line 27) should either be removed or updated to describe the actual ordering of indicators that ARE added. The data flow diagram on line 52 should remove the `→ _build_indicators appends 🥞 when is_stacked is True` line.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Fixed in commit ef39d66 — updated line 27 to note the pancake emoji is not currently added by _build_indicators() (is_stacked is a no-op), and updated line 52 data flow diagram to reflect this.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-25 03:32 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy that is now inaccurate.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py:232-233`

This code block shows:
```
has_blocking_indicators = any(i != "🥞" for i in indicators)
```

But the source after this PR uses:
```python
_non_blocking = {"🥞", "👀", "✔"}
has_blocking_indicators = any(i not in _non_blocking for i in indicators)
```

The 👀 (published indicator) and ✔ (approved indicator) are now also non-blocking, so the single-emoji exclusion check shown in the doc is stale.

Suggested fix: Replace with the source pointer format from `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md` (HTML comment + prose reference). A source pointer is already present on line 48 — fold the description into prose:
> The mechanism uses an exclusion check against a non-blocking set (🥞, 👀, ✔): if any indicator is not in this set, the plan has blocking indicators and the rocket is suppressed.

Then remove the code block entirely.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Python**: Import organization - combine imports from the same module on lines 24-29 into a single import statement to follow import organization standards.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
False positive: ruff isort rules enforce separate import statements when an alias (as) is present. Combining these imports causes ruff I001 violation, which ruff --fix reverts. The imports are already in the correct ruff-enforced style.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 23:12 UTC_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Python Review**: No re-export gratuitous aliasing - Imports must use canonical names. The 'as' keyword should only be used for resolving genuine name collisions between different modules. Use 'require_issues' directly rather than aliasing to 'require_github_issues'.

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
Removed gratuitous alias, now using canonical name require_issues directly in fd6062b8e
